actions.text: # DETAILED_RECAP
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window
Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: Feedback**: - [ ] Useful
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window
Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: ## Key Decisions and Agreements
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: **Pivot employer outreach to "candidate cards" approach** — Alex recommended creating digestible "playing card" style candidate summaries (picture, 2-3 experience points, 2-3 story snippets) delivered via trackable URL bundles. This breaks the mold of resume-heavy recruiter outreach and provides instant value to hiring managers.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: **Target hiring managers directly, not HR/talent teams** — Rationale: HR likes traditional resume review processes (job security), but busy team leads/engineering managers will respond better to human-centric, pre-filtered candidate presentations. Hiring managers have authority to skip ATS anyway.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: **Implement "goods up front" sales approach** — Send candidate bundles immediately without building relationship first. Alex noted recruiters waste time trying to establish rapport before showing candidates, which is annoying. Lead with value: "Here are candidates, do you want to pay for them?"
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: **Use URL hosting for engagement metrics** — Track click-through rates, profiles browsed, time spent to optimize outreach strategy. Each URL is unique per employer to gather performance data.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: **Categorize candidates thematically, not percentage fit scores** — Sections like "Clear Fits" (on-paper matches) and "Hidden Gems" (career transitioners, atypical profiles) feel more curated and human than algorithmic percentage scores.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: ## Strategic Context
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: Product Positioning**: Careerspan has shifted from selling behavioral insights (too high burden of proof, buyers jaded) to a staffing play: "If we get good candidates in and introduce them to employers, we make money." This is more objective and testable.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: Current GTM**: Targeting product management roles in NYC/SF/Boston. Using "magic link" system (candidates apply through Careerspan), embedding in communities, reaching out to high-quality candidates directly. Employers agreed to $300-400 per warm intro, contingent on seeing quality.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: Product Strength**: Alex praised the coaching interface simplicity and story accumulation mechanic (candidates tell stories to fill gaps, stories persist across applications). Noted this creates defensible candidate data that traditional recruiters can't replicate. The nuanced chatbot implementation (speeds up/slows down based on response quality) impressed technical users.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: Hiring Manager Pain Point**: Resume review is "mind-numbingly boring" and time-consuming. Product managers especially have varied backgrounds (career transitioners, IC-to-PM pivots), making atypical profiles harder to evaluate. Careerspan's story-based insights surface "what the resume misses."
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: Pitch Event Context**: Vrijen preparing for Atlanta pitch event ($2k entry, founder referral). Alex advised pitch events are typically low-value for customers but excellent for founder practice (confidence projection, timing, conciseness). Vrijen acknowledged need to be "front-footed" after previous insecurity about product quality—now has data showing strong engagement metrics.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: Purpose**: Validate whether "goods up front" + playing card UX converts hiring managers better than traditional recruiter outreach. This unlocks all GTM channels (community partnerships, cold outreach, subscription model).
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: **Candidate card system**: Logan and I are prototyping the "playing card" style bundles (experience + stories, trackable URLs) as the core employer deliverable
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: **Hiring manager targeting**: Shifting outreach away from HR/talent teams toward team leads who prioritize efficiency over process
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: **Thematic categorization**: Testing "Clear Fits" and "Hidden Gems" groupings instead of percentage scores to feel more curated
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: **Job posting age segmentation**: Building pipeline to test fresh vs. stale posting timing for outreach
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: I'll update you once we have the candidate card prototype live—would love your gut check on whether it hits the "digestible and enticing" bar you described
